Paper.title: Dead lithium formation in lithium metal batteries: A phase field model

Model.name: Galvanostatic phase-field model for lithium stripping with dead lithium formation
Model.description: A two-phase electrochemical phase-field model for lithium plating/stripping that explicitly models dead lithium formation during stripping under galvanostatic conditions.
Model.evidence: The paper states that it proposes a phase field model to describe the lithium stripping process with dead lithium formation, and that galvanostatic charge/discharge conditions are realized.

ModelVariant.name: Chen nonlinear phase-field model
ModelVariant.description: Prior Butler-Volmer-coupled nonlinear electrochemical phase-field model used as the theoretical basis for the present formulation.
ModelVariant.evidence: The paper states the present model is based on Chen's nonlinear phase field model and previous works.

Equation.name: Lithium plating/stripping reaction
Equation.description: Electrochemical reaction underlying lithium deposition and dissolution.
Equation.evidence: The modeling section introduces the electrochemical reaction during lithium plating and stripping as Li+ + e- ↔ Li.

Equation.name: Total free energy functional
Equation.expression: F = ∫_V [f_ch(c_i) + f_grad(n) + f_elec(c_i, u) + f_ns(n)] dV
Equation.description: Defines the total free energy as the sum of chemical, gradient, electrostatic, and noise contributions.
Equation.evidence: Equation (2) defines the total free energy using chemical, gradient, electrostatic, and noise energy densities.

Equation.name: Lithium plating phase-field evolution equation
Equation.description: Describes the temporal evolution of the phase-field order parameter during lithium plating with Butler-Volmer kinetics.
Equation.evidence: Equation (3) gives the PDE for lithium plating based on interfacial mobility, reaction kinetics, and noise contribution.

Equation.name: Lithium stripping phase-field evolution equation
Equation.expression: ∂n/∂t = f_d Lσ(∂f0/∂n - κ∇²n + ∂f_ns/∂n) - f_d Lη h'(n)[exp((1-α)Fη/RT) - a_Li+ exp(-αFη/RT)]
Equation.description: Describes lithium stripping while modulating reaction activity through an active-state function that distinguishes active lithium from dead lithium.
Equation.evidence: Equation (4) introduces fd as a variable describing the active state of local lithium metal, with fd = 0 denoting dead lithium.

Equation.name: Lithium-ion concentration evolution equation
Equation.description: Describes lithium-ion diffusion, migration, and coupling to phase transformation.
Equation.evidence: Equation (5) gives the temporal evolution of Li ion concentration including diffusion, migration, and a source term coupled to phase evolution.

Equation.name: Electrolyte potential equation
Equation.description: Poisson/Laplace equation used to solve the liquid-phase electric potential.
Equation.evidence: Equation (6) states that the electric potential in the electrolyte liquid phase ul can be solved with Poisson's equation ∇²ul = 0.

Equation.name: Galvanostatic constraint
Equation.description: Enforces constant areal current density by matching imposed current, reaction current, and mass-transfer current.
Equation.evidence: Equation (7) defines the galvanostatic simulation condition by requiring I0, Ir, and Imt to remain equal throughout plating/stripping.

Equation.name: Reaction areal current density
Equation.description: Computes reaction current density from phase evolution.
Equation.evidence: Equation (8) defines reaction areal current density in terms of the phase-field evolution.

Equation.name: Mass-transfer areal current density
Equation.expression: Imt = -(F / S) ∫_S n·(D_Li+∇c_Li+ + D_Li+ c_Li+ (F/RT)∇u_l) dS
Equation.description: Computes mass-transfer current density from ionic flux at the boundary.
Equation.evidence: Equation (9) defines mass transfer areal current density from Li-ion diffusion and migration fluxes.

Term.name: chemical free energy density
Term.description: Helmholtz free energy density of the chemical species.
Term.evidence: The paper defines fch as the Helmholtz free energy density involving concentrations, activities, and reference chemical potentials.

Term.name: gradient energy density
Term.description: Interfacial gradient energy density controlled by an anisotropic gradient coefficient.
Term.evidence: The paper defines fgrad and states that the gradient coefficient is associated with surface energy anisotropy.

Term.name: electrostatic energy density
Term.description: Electrostatic energy contribution involving species concentrations, potential, Faraday constant, and valence.
Term.evidence: The paper defines felec as the electrostatic energy density.

Term.name: noise term
Term.evidence: The paper defines fns as the noise term presenting the non-uniform interface.

Term.name: double-well potential
Term.description: Double-well free-energy function distinguishing electrode and electrolyte equilibrium states.
Term.evidence: The paper defines f0(n) as an arbitrary double well function where W/16 is the barrier height between the two equilibrium states.

Term.name: interpolating function
Term.description: Interpolation function used in Butler-Volmer-coupled phase evolution.
Term.evidence: The paper defines h(n) as an interpolating function in the phase-field evolution equations.

Term.name: active-state function
Term.description: Step-function-based activity variable used to distinguish active lithium from dead lithium during stripping.
Term.evidence: The paper introduces fd as a variable to describe the active state of local lithium metal, with fd = 1 for active lithium and fd = 0 for dead lithium.

Term.name: Li-ion activity during stripping
Term.description: Lithium-ion activity used in the stripping reaction term when concentration exceeds dilute-solution assumptions.
Term.evidence: The paper introduces aLi+ as the activity of Li ions because Li-ion concentration is higher than 1.0 M during stripping.

Variable.name: phase-field order parameter
Variable.physical_meaning: Describes the phase evolution between electrolyte and lithium metal.
Variable.evidence: The paper states that n = 0 denotes electrolyte liquid phase and n = 1 denotes lithium metal solid phase.

Variable.name: species concentration
Variable.physical_meaning: Concentration of species i such as Li ions, anions, and Li atoms.
Variable.evidence: The paper defines ci in the chemical free energy density as the concentrations of species i.

Variable.name: electrolyte liquid-phase potential
Variable.physical_meaning: Electric potential in the electrolyte liquid phase.
Variable.evidence: The paper states that the electric potential in the electrolyte liquid phase ul is solved using Poisson's equation.

Variable.name: solid-phase potential
Variable.physical_meaning: Electric potential in the lithium metal solid phase.
Variable.evidence: The paper states that the electrostatic potential in the lithium metal solid phase us is considered equal everywhere in the solid phase and evolves over time.

Variable.name: activation overpotential
Variable.physical_meaning: Driving force for electrochemical plating/stripping reaction.
Variable.evidence: The paper defines η = us - ul - Eeq as the activation overpotential.

Variable.name: lithium-ion concentration
Variable.evidence: The paper gives a dedicated PDE for the temporal evolution of Li-ion concentration.

Variable.name: active-state indicator
Variable.physical_meaning: Indicator of whether local lithium is electrochemically active or dead.
Variable.evidence: The paper defines fd = 1 as active lithium and fd = 0 as dead lithium.

Variable.name: lithium-ion activity
Variable.physical_meaning: Effective activity of Li ions used in stripping kinetics.
Variable.evidence: The paper introduces aLi+ as the activity of Li ions during stripping.

Variable.name: imposed areal current density
Variable.physical_meaning: Input galvanostatic current density for charging/discharging.
Variable.evidence: The paper states that I0 is the constant areal current density input parameter, taking positive and negative values during charging and discharging.

Variable.name: reaction areal current density
Variable.physical_meaning: Current density associated with phase transformation reaction.
Variable.evidence: The paper defines Ir as the reaction areal current density.

Variable.name: mass-transfer areal current density
Variable.physical_meaning: Current density associated with ionic transport.
Variable.evidence: The paper defines Imt as the mass transfer areal current density.

Variable.name: dead lithium capacity
Variable.physical_meaning: Capacity corresponding to electrically disconnected lithium.
Variable.evidence: Figure 4d and the discussion define the dead lithium capacity curve Qdead.

Variable.name: active lithium capacity
Variable.physical_meaning: Capacity corresponding to electrochemically active lithium.
Variable.evidence: Figure 4d and the discussion define the active lithium capacity curve Qactive.

Variable.name: capacity loss rate
Variable.physical_meaning: First derivative of dead lithium capacity with respect to time.
Variable.evidence: The paper defines Iloss as the first derivative of dead lithium capacity with respect to time.

Parameter.name: interfacial mobility
Parameter.physical_meaning: Controls interface evolution contribution in the phase-field equations.
Parameter.evidence: The paper states that Lσ denotes the interfacial mobility.

Parameter.name: reaction constant
Parameter.physical_meaning: Controls electrochemical reaction kinetics in the phase-field equations.
Parameter.evidence: The paper states that Lη denotes the reaction constant.

Parameter.name: gradient energy coefficient
Parameter.physical_meaning: Controls interfacial gradient energy and anisotropy effects.
Parameter.evidence: The paper defines the gradient coefficient in the gradient energy density and expands it with anisotropy.

Parameter.name: baseline gradient energy coefficient
Parameter.physical_meaning: Reference gradient energy coefficient in anisotropic expansion.
Parameter.evidence: The paper defines κ0 as the gradient energy coefficient in the anisotropy expansion.

Parameter.name: anisotropy strength
Parameter.evidence: The paper defines δ as the anisotropy strength.

Parameter.name: anisotropy mode
Parameter.physical_meaning: Mode number in the anisotropic gradient coefficient.
Parameter.evidence: The paper defines ω as the anisotropy mode.

Parameter.name: interface normal angle
Parameter.physical_meaning: Relative angle of the interface normal vector.
Parameter.evidence: The paper defines θ as the relative angle of the interface normal vector.

Parameter.name: Faraday constant
Parameter.physical_meaning: Electrochemical constant linking charge and mole transfer.
Parameter.evidence: The paper defines F as Faraday's constant.

Parameter.name: molar gas constant
Parameter.evidence: The paper defines R as the molar gas constant.

Parameter.name: temperature
Parameter.physical_meaning: Thermal state entering kinetic and free-energy relations.
Parameter.evidence: The paper states that T is the temperature.

Parameter.name: barrier height parameter
Parameter.physical_meaning: Barrier height between electrode and electrolyte equilibrium states.
Parameter.evidence: The paper states that W/16 represents the barrier height between the two equilibrium states.

Parameter.name: charge transfer coefficient
Parameter.physical_meaning: Cathodic/anodic transfer coefficient in Butler-Volmer kinetics.
Parameter.evidence: The paper defines 1-α and α as the anodic and cathodic charge transfer coefficients.

Parameter.name: Li-ion diffusion coefficient
Parameter.physical_meaning: Diffusion coefficient of lithium ions in electrolyte.
Parameter.evidence: The paper states that DLi+ is the Li ion diffusion coefficient in electrolytes.

Parameter.name: Li atom concentration in solid phase
Parameter.physical_meaning: Lithium atom concentration in lithium metal solid phase.
Parameter.evidence: The paper states that cs is the Li atom concentration in the lithium metal solid phase.

Parameter.name: reference potential for activity cutoff
Parameter.physical_meaning: Threshold potential used to determine local active/dead lithium state.
Parameter.evidence: The paper sets the reference potential ud as 1.0 mV for identifying active and dead lithium.

Parameter.name: reference Li-ion concentration
Parameter.physical_meaning: Bulk/reference electrolyte concentration used for normalization.
Parameter.evidence: The paper defines c0 as the constant Li ion concentration at the upper boundary and uses it in the kinetic terms.

Parameter.name: random number
Parameter.physical_meaning: Random fluctuation variable in the noise term.
Parameter.evidence: The paper states that v is the random number in the noise term.

Parameter.name: fluctuation amplitude
Parameter.physical_meaning: Amplitude of fluctuation in the noise term.
Parameter.evidence: The paper states that w is the amplitude of fluctuation.

ParameterValue.condition: reference potential for active/dead lithium discrimination
ParameterValue.evidence: The paper states that the reference potential ud is set as 1.0 mV.
The paper states that a two-dimensional model consisting of a 30.0 μm × 30.0 μm square domain is introduced.
The paper states that the maximum grid spacing is 0.5 μm.

ParameterValue.condition: example plating current density in Fig. 2
ParameterValue.evidence: Figure 2 caption states a plating current density of 10 mA cm^-2.

ParameterValue.condition: example stripping current density in Fig. 2
ParameterValue.evidence: Figure 2 caption states a stripping current density of 10 mA cm^-2.

ParameterValue.condition: computed Coulombic efficiency for demonstrated case
ParameterValue.evidence: The paper states that the Coulombic efficiency can be directly calculated as 83.1%.

Phenomenon.name: dead lithium formation
Phenomenon.description: Formation of electrically disconnected metallic lithium during stripping.
Phenomenon.evidence: The paper focuses on dead lithium formation during lithium stripping.

Phenomenon.name: lithium dendrite growth
Phenomenon.description: Growth of dendritic lithium morphology during plating.
Phenomenon.evidence: The paper first generates dendritic lithium plating morphology as a prerequisite for dead lithium formation.

Phenomenon.name: uneven lithium stripping
Phenomenon.description: Nonuniform dissolution of lithium dendrites leading to detachment and dead lithium.
Phenomenon.evidence: The paper states that lithium dendrites tend to dissolve unevenly during stripping.

Phenomenon.name: cell polarization increase
Phenomenon.description: Increase in activation overpotential and total cell polarization caused by dead lithium formation.
Phenomenon.evidence: The paper concludes that dead lithium formation significantly increases activation overpotential and cell polarization.

Phenomenon.name: capacity loss
Phenomenon.description: Irreversible cycle-capacity loss caused by dead lithium formation.
Phenomenon.evidence: The paper states that dead lithium formation is directly responsible for loss of cycle capacity.

MaterialSystem.description: Lithium metal battery half-cell system with lithium metal anode and electrolyte containing Li ions.
MaterialSystem.evidence: The model considers a two-phase system composed of lithium metal solid phase and electrolyte liquid phase containing lithium ions.

Geometry.description: Finite-element simulation domain used for galvanostatic plating/stripping.
Geometry.evidence: The paper introduces a two-dimensional 30.0 μm × 30.0 μm square domain.

Condition.description: Constant areal current density enforced throughout plating and stripping.
Condition.evidence: The paper emphasizes galvanostatic charge/discharge simulation conditions.

Condition.description: Upper boundary treated as bulk electrolyte with fixed lithium-ion concentration.
Condition.evidence: The paper states that the upper boundary is considered as bulk electrolyte with constant Li ion concentration c0.

Condition.description: Lower boundary acts as current collector with liquid-phase potential fixed to zero.
Condition.evidence: The paper states that the lower boundary acts as a current collector where ul is set as 0.

Result.description: The proposed model simulates dead lithium formation during galvanostatic lithium stripping.
Result.evidence: The paper reports simulated dead lithium formation during the galvanostatic lithium stripping process.

Result.description: Dead lithium formation starts from upper dendrite regions and detached pieces adopt the surrounding electrolyte potential, making them electrochemically inactive.
Result.evidence: The paper states that dead lithium generation starts from the top and that dead lithium has the same electrostatic potential as the surrounding electrolyte, so it cannot transmit electrons to the current collector.

Result.description: Dead lithium formation reduces active lithium surface area, which sharply increases local reaction rate, activation overpotential, and cell polarization under galvanostatic discharge.
Result.condition: dead lithium formation under galvanostatic discharge
Result.evidence: The paper concludes that reduction of active lithium surface area and rising local Li-ion concentration significantly intensify activation overpotential and polarization.

Result.description: Li-ion concentration near dendrites increases rapidly during stripping and further aggravates overpotential growth.
Result.evidence: The paper states that Li ion concentration around lithium dendrites increases rapidly during stripping and aggravates the increase of activation overpotential.

Result.description: The first primary dead-lithium piece forms at 250.5 s in the demonstrated case.
Result.evidence: The paper states that the first piece of dead lithium is formed at 250.5 s.

Result.description: Active lithium is fully consumed at 404.8 s, terminating stripping in the demonstrated case.
Result.evidence: The paper states that when discharge time reaches 404.8 s, all active lithium is consumed and the simulation ends automatically.

Result.description: The model reproduces total voltage, average activation overpotential, active lithium capacity, dead lithium capacity, and capacity loss rate during stripping.
Result.evidence: The paper reports simulation of the battery polarization curve, capacity loss peak, and Coulomb efficiency, and Figure 4 shows these quantities.

Result.description: Loss peaks in the capacity-loss-rate spectrum correspond one-to-one with dead-lithium formation events and with potential jumps in the polarization curve.
Result.evidence: The paper states that the loss peaks clearly identify dead lithium formation time points and correspond one-to-one with the potential step time points.

Result.description: The Coulombic efficiency for the demonstrated case is 83.1%.
Result.condition: simulated example including initial nucleation sites
Result.evidence: The paper states that the Coulombic efficiency can be directly calculated as 83.1%.

Metric.name: Coulombic efficiency
Metric.description: Ratio of recoverable active lithium capacity to plated lithium capacity.
Metric.evidence: The paper reports Coulombic efficiency and calculates it as 83.1% in the demonstrated case.

Metric.name: total polarization / total voltage
Metric.description: Overall voltage response of the half-cell under galvanostatic stripping.
Metric.evidence: The paper states that the total voltage corresponds to the total polarization in the half-cell model.

Metric.name: average activation overpotential
Metric.description: Average interfacial overpotential driving the electrochemical reaction.
Metric.evidence: The paper analyzes the average activation overpotential and its increase during dead lithium formation.

Metric.name: capacity loss rate
Metric.description: Time derivative of dead lithium capacity, used to identify dead-lithium formation events.
Metric.evidence: The paper defines Iloss as the first derivative of dead lithium capacity with respect to time.
